frameworks.name: multisensory cortical integration
frameworks.one_line: view that V1 directly encodes non-visual modalities such as sound

frameworks.name: behavior-driven cortical activity
frameworks.one_line: cortex-wide activity arises from movement and internal state, not pure sensory drive

frameworks.name: low-dimensional population coding
frameworks.one_line: stereotyped low-dimensional patterns shared across neurons and animals

core_question: Does sound-evoked activity in mouse V1 reflect genuine multisensory integration or movement-related signals elicited by the sounds?
key_assumptions: Neuropixels recordings sample V1 population activity faithfully
small body movements can be tracked and used as predictors
auditory cortex provides the candidate direct projection to V1
manipulations: presenting sounds while recording V1 with Neuropixels
comparing V1 with hippocampal formation responses
silencing or removing auditory cortical projections
regressing neural activity on stimulus-elicited body movements
key_findings: sound-evoked V1 activity is stereotyped across neurons and across mice
this activity does not depend on auditory cortical projections
it resembles activity in hippocampal formation, which lacks direct auditory input
small sound-elicited body movements precisely predict the V1 sound response
interpretations: apparent multisensory responses in V1 reflect movement and internal-state signals, not direct auditory input
low-dimensional behavior-linked signals can masquerade as cross-modal sensory coding
